encounter.discharge_destination: Usual place of residence with safety-net advice

encounter_summary: Acute calculous cholecystitis: Persistent right upper-quadrant pain, fever and vomiting beginning after an evening meal. Antibiotics and total cholecystectomy after surgical review and consent. Pain controlled, oral intake restored and discharge advice understood.

timeline.title: Episode opened
timeline.detail: Persistent right upper-quadrant pain, fever and vomiting beginning after an evening meal.

timeline.title: Assessment complete
timeline.detail: Ultrasound shows gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening and pericholecystic fluid.

timeline.title: Discharged
timeline.detail: Pain controlled, oral intake restored and discharge advice understood.

passages.text: Persistent right upper-quadrant pain, fever and vomiting beginning after an evening meal.
Right upper-quadrant tenderness and a positive Murphy sign without jaundice.
Clinical impression: Gallbladder calculus with acute cholecystitis.
Ultrasound shows gallstones, gallbladder wall thickening and pericholecystic fluid.
Procedure completed: Total cholecystectomy. No immediate complication documented.
Antibiotics and total cholecystectomy after surgical review and consent.
Pain controlled, oral intake restored and discharge advice understood.
Synthetic record only. Review trigger reference 911; not for patient care.

procedure_note: A classified intervention is documented for this episode.

codes.display: Calculus of gallbladder with acute cholecystitis
codes.rationale: The episode documentation supports K80.0 (Calculus of gallbladder with acute cholecystitis).
The documented procedure supports J18.3 (Total cholecystectomy NEC).
